name: Atelier Modules
label: Atelier Modules
description: Baseline Atelier modules, including: Horse Browser and auth for agents, gwx, Status Bar, and dev setup.

apps.name: Status Bar
apps.tagline: A name for every agent, and the terminal line that wears it.
apps.description: **Session Codes + the Claude Code status bar.** Every session earns a tiny identity — callsign, colour, emoji — from one hash the terminal, the dashboard, and the browser all agree on. Part 01 teaches the identity engine with a live rolling decoder and the 48-codename grid; part 02 dissects the statusline piece by piece, then wires it into `~/.claude/settings.json` with one click (merged, never clobbered — backed up first). The page honestly reports what's wired: this module's script, the same codename script from another copy, a different statusline, or nothing.

apps.name: Horse Browser
apps.tagline: Give your agents a browser of their own — logged in, and never in your way.
apps.description: **The night console.** A second browser just for agents — signed in once, quietly shared, never stealing your screen. Built on browser-harness (~600 lines of raw-CDP engine vs ~120k for Playwright — the bitter lesson of agent harnesses) and the dedicated Horse Browser, installed and updated from npm (`@pa1nd/horse-browser`). The page shows a live wall of your real stack: agent sessions, the harness daemons driving them, and the actual tabs they have open — name-matched by session codename.

apps.name: CLAUDE.md
apps.tagline: A note Claude reads before it writes a line.
apps.description: **The note Claude reads first.** The four rules that went viral — Think Before Coding, Simplicity First, Surgical Changes, Goal-Driven Execution — with the story of where they came from, and a modal that renders the full skill. Your real `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` is read live, chapter by chapter, with the four-rules chapter lit when present; one click appends the block (never clobbers, always backed up). Also tracks the Horse Browser playbooks import that shares the same file.

apps.name: gwx
apps.tagline: Your whole Google Workspace, in your agent's hands.
apps.description: **One multi-account command over your whole Google Workspace.** Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets and more — reads fan out across every account in parallel, and a library of 96 ready-made agent skills (per-service helpers, multi-step recipes, whole personas) loads on demand, browsable in a searchable catalogue. A skill + a CLI beats an MCP: the agent gets the whole command surface, not a hand-picked menu. Installed and updated from npm (`@pa1nd/gwx`), with live per-account sign-in state.

apps.name: HB Auth
apps.tagline: Credentials your agents can use but never see.
apps.description: **The credentials & login-methods manager for agents.** An account registry / recipe book over your vault, and the ENFORCED Bitwarden broker — a signed local daemon that holds the only vault session and gates every credential behind per-credential policy, an origin check read from the browser, and a native macOS approval. Secrets are typed straight into the page over CDP, so they never enter the model's context. Ships the older LastPass + SMS-2FA convention path too. macOS only; the broker compiles on first run (Swift / Xcode CLT) and the Bitwarden CLI does the vault I/O.

apps.name: HB Display
apps.tagline: Screenshots that survive a closed lid.
apps.description: **The Horse Browser's display & health wing.** With the display asleep — especially clamshell — WindowServer composites nothing and agent screenshots hang. This page answers the headline question with a REAL probe (a timed 1×1 CDP screenshot), shows the display census (asleep / clamshell / virtual displays), installs and launches DeskPad (audited: 436 lines, sandboxed, no network entitlement) for a virtual display that never sleeps, and streams the launcher's self-heal journal live. macOS only.

apps.name: Atelier Config
apps.tagline: Your whole instance, edited from inside it.
apps.description: **The instance's settings panel.** atelier.config.json as a friendly form or a highlighted raw-JSON editor — saves are staged, shape-validated, written atomically, and patch-merged so a concurrent edit is never clobbered (a drifted key rejects as a conflict instead). Manage apps & workspaces by drag-and-drop (link/unlink path-mounts, create/rename/delete workspaces, chrome-coloured lanes), check the system's package managers against the latest LTS / stable upstream, run the instance at login as a macOS LaunchAgent (with a guided port handoff from your dev server), and watch the server's real output stream live. No client polling — launchd state and log tails arrive over the shell WebSocket.

apps.name: Module Development
apps.tagline: Set up for serious module development — three folders, three jobs.
apps.description: **Set your instance up for serious module development.** Instance folder (the wiring: config, .env, shell), modules folder (the workshop — agents build here), chromes folder (the themes — hands off from module tasks). The instance and chromes folders get CLAUDE.md playbooks (the modules folder stays clean — it’s just a container), filled with YOUR instance's real paths — existing files are backed up and appended to, never clobbered, and every template is previewable first. A live checklist reads your instance (folders, installPath wiring, playbook states, honestly reported), and a migration scan lists what still lives in the wrong folder with a copyable step-by-step agent brief. Works right after a fresh install — or as the guided migration for an instance that's been running for a while.

apps.name: Catalyst Chrome
apps.tagline: The Catalyst-based theme these modules render in.
apps.description: The **chrome** the collection's modules pin (`meta.chrome = 'catalyst-chrome'`) — a Tailwind Catalyst-based theme that owns the rail, topbar, tokens, and dark mode, and publishes the `@atelier/kit` primitives (Button, Dialog, Table, …) companion modules import.
